description: Direct personal testimonial of venue experience combining location tagging, service quality assessment, and specific product feedback to build social proof and authenticity.
structural_recipe: Tag the venue location with a distinctive landmark (e.g., Via Riyadh). Open with venue name and emoji category indicator. Include atmosphere/ambiance assessment (راقي - elegant/sophisticated). Evaluate service quality explicitly (ممتازة - excellent). Provide specific menu item feedback with sensory description (taste, quality). Use mix of Arabic and English for premium positioning. Tag both brand and location accounts.
why_it_works: Saudi audiences value authentic peer recommendations over branded messaging. Specific sensory details (لذيذ - delicious) combined with service quality metrics build trust. Location tagging with premium area association (Via Riyadh) signals lifestyle alignment. Arabic-English code-switching positions content as sophisticated/cosmopolitan while remaining locally relevant.
transplantation_caveats: Authenticity is critical - fabricated feedback detected quickly by Saudi audiences
Service quality feedback must be balanced; overly negative content may face pushback
Premium venue association necessary for engagement; budget venues perform differently
Sensory language must match actual product quality or credibility collapses
cultural_constraints: Ensure venue respects Islamic values and gender-segregation norms if applicable
Avoid overly casual tone when discussing premium establishments
Respectful of hospitality standards valued in Saudi culture
